## Source Record

- Source URL: `https://chainlit.io/`
- Fetch method: `webfetch` in markdown mode
- Capture date: `2026-04-14`

## Why It Matters

This page positions Chainlit as an application framework around conversational AI delivery. It is useful for understanding how Chainlit presents the runtime envelope around a Python chat app: local execution, standalone web deployment, embedded copilots, server modes, bot integrations, authentication, and ecosystem adapters.

## Key Excerpts

> "Build AI applications"

> "$ pip install chainlit"

> "$ chainlit run app.py"

> "Deploy your app as a standalone web app, an embedded copilot, FastAPI server, custom React or Slack/Discord/Teams bot."

## Capture Notes

- The homepage is marketing-heavy, but it still exposes important runtime signals: install path, run command, deployment targets, auth, customization, and integrations.
- The page explicitly names integrations with OpenAI, Mistral, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and HuggingFace.
- This is a product overview, not a technical API reference.
